macro_summary: Thursday’s session closed mixed: the Dow surged 875 points to a record, the S&P 500 added 0.9% to 757.09, but tech slumped on Broadcom’s 14% post-earnings rout after its AI chip forecast missed estimates. TSMC’s CEO warned chip supply can’t meet AI demand for years, while WTI eased 1.7% to $93.47 despite overnight Persian Gulf hostilities. Fresh tariff proposals on 60 nations over forced‑labor claims added trade uncertainty.
commodities_note: WTI fell to $93.47 (-1.7%) even as ships remained stuck inside Hormuz (Day 95); gold dipped 0.4% to $4,472.60; the US 10‑year yield ticked down to 4.48% but held near the top of its range after strong ADP payrolls data.

thesis_highlights: ~45% of MSFT $625B commercial RPO is OpenAI-linked — non-OpenAI commercial RPO (+28%) is the cleaner demand signal.

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS, but momentum_warn is True (weak recent tape). ACCUMULATE requires either an explicit multi-year structural support cite (200-day SMA / prior cycle low / named floor) OR an imminent hard catalyst within 7 days (specific date). Generic fundamental praise does NOT clear the bar — default to WATCH if neither anchor applies.

writeup.headline: ACCUMULATE MSFT — Azure non‑OpenAI RPO is the clean read, R:R 2.8:1, starter position.
writeup.what_to_do: ACCUMULATE with a starter position. Entry zone $425–430, invalidation at $406.74 (50‑day SMA break). Headline R:R to $486.97 is 2.8:1, but nearest resistance at $432.26 (1% above) means the trade only materializes after that level breaks on volume. Wide‑stop R:R using structural support at $398.58 is 2.0:1, still attractive. Microsoft’s commercial RPO ex‑OpenAI (+28% YoY) is the cleaner demand signal as Azure AI revenue quality remains under scrutiny. Half position given OpenAI concentration fragility. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.
writeup.valuation_judgment: Trades at a 13.7% discount to the BigTech cluster median PE, reflecting the Azure OpenAI credit circularity discount — a reasonable entry for the non‑OpenAI growth story.
writeup.tight_upside_disclosure: Nearest resistance at $432.26 (1% above) — headline R:R only materialises once that level breaks on above‑average volume.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if Azure revenue quality — OpenAI credit circularity deteriorates materially.

support_legs: Azure commercial RPO ex-OpenAI (+28% YoY) signals organic enterprise AI demand independent of OpenAI credits
New in-house AI models and Copilot monetization pipeline provide independent growth vectors not tied to OpenAI

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain extended and overbought, with no relief from the dual NIM headwind. The Simba‑M1 deal collapse adds sector‑crowding risk but isn’t a direct earnings driver. OCBC is the relative outperformer on profit growth, but that alone can’t overcome the MAS S$NEER + US10Y ceiling.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — dual rate/FX compression persists, no catalyst until October MAS review or a sustained US10Y break below 4.20%.
singapore.key_development: No new earnings or policy catalyst; the cluster’s uniform CAUTION regime enters its 71st day, signaling a durable structural headwind rather than a short‑term tactical pullback.

semiconductors.summary: The Broadcom AI chip forecast miss (14% single‑day rout) ended a three‑day record streak, dragging SOXX 2.1% lower. TSMC’s supply warning underscores the structural demand, but immediate sentiment is fractured. Foundry names (INTC, LITE) held up better than memory (MU, SK Hynix), highlighting an intra‑cluster divergence between capex‑driven capacity and consumer‑sensitive memory.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but tactically extended; the Broadcom miss is a stall in AI hype, not a reversal, but 12 of 12 semis names remain blocked on extension — the sector’s beta is the lever here, not stock selection.
semiconductors.key_development: Broadcom’s AI chip revenue guide of $16B vs. $17.2B consensus triggered profit‑taking across the cluster, with AMD, INTC, and MU all down 3%–7% in sympathy despite no company‑specific news.

big_tech.summary: GOOG rallied 4.1% on AI deal momentum, while MSFT held near its 50‑day with the ACCUMULATE signal intact. Amazon’s tight stop keeps it in HOLD, and PLTR’s defense AI story struggles to overcome a 68x PE. The cluster is drifting, but the capex commitment ($5.3T through 2030 per Goldman) anchors the long‑term thesis.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — Azure re‑accelerating but OpenAI concentration lingers; capex sustainability debate heightened after AVGO miss, but BigTech’s ability to self‑fund AI infrastructure differentiates it from the semis cluster.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT is the standout as the only BigTech name in ACCUMULATE territory, with a clean non‑OpenAI RPO read and a favorable R:R, despite volume caution.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Second leg of NVIDIA’s $2B AI photonics investment (alongside LITE); directly tied to LITE’s Greensboro fab build‑out and AI datacenter optical interconnects.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Coherent holds a breakout above $X (price to be determined after next pipeline run) on confirmed NVDA capacity‑rights news, or if LITE’s pullback stabilizes.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Primary beneficiary of AI datacenter switching upgrades (800G/1.6T) within the NVIDIA Spectrum‑X ecosystem; demand correlates with MSFT/GOOG capex and hyperscaler networking buildouts.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for a 5–8% pullback from current levels and cross‑reference with MSFT’s next Azure capex commentary before considering a watchlist add.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and AI‑networking play competing directly with AVGO; benefits from the same hyperscaler shift toward custom silicon (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) that drives AVGO’s order book.
interconnected.entry_note: Only becomes interesting if AVGO’s weakness presents a relative‑value opportunity — monitor MRVL’s custom ASIC design‑win pipeline on its next earnings call, and pullback‑to‑SMA50 entry.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz paralysis (Day 95): Oil tankers remain trapped, insurance costs soar; no ceasefire breakthrough despite ongoing negotiations.
US tariff escalation: Trump proposes 10‑12.5% tariffs on 60 nations over forced‑labor claims, risking fresh trade friction with EU and Japan.
Federal Reserve hawkish stance: Strong ADP jobs report and elevated inflation (CPI 3.9% in April) keep rates near 3.62% with a bias to hold or hike.
China economic softening: Private factory gauge slows; Iran war supply shocks raise input costs for Chinese manufacturers, adding to trade tension.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil remains elevated near $93, supporting defense and energy names (PLTR, BE) but pressuring consumer stocks. High US10Y yields (4.48%) keep Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) under dual margin compression. Semis are selling off on AVGO’s miss, but the cluster’s structural demand argument is intact; the correction is a tactical breather, not a regime change.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold defensive posture; no new entries into extended names. Continue to accumulate MSFT on pullbacks, and monitor AVGO’s post‑earnings reset for a potential WATCH upgrade if the 50‑day holds.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously bullish — extension dominates the watchlist, but valuation and structural AI demand provide a floor.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 17 of 27 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension, leaving only a handful of actionable setups (MSFT ACCUMULATE, WRD WATCH) amid a sector‑wide pullback from overbought levels.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Payrolls miss consensus (e.g., <100k), wage growth cools → US10Y drops below 4.40%, easing NIM pressure and potentially triggering a relief rally for Singapore banks.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Payrolls beat (e.g., >200k), wages surprise upward → US10Y breaks above 4.55%, extending rate‑stress and keeping all bank names in CAUTION deep into Q2.
bearish_impact: MSFT ACCUMULATE may be paused (VIX risk)

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Core CPI eases to 3.6% or below → Fed rate‑cut hopes revive, US10Y dips, lifting semis and easing extension blocks.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI stays hot at 3.9%+ → Fed rate‑hike narrative intensifies, US10Y above 4.6%, crushing rate‑sensitive names and keeping semis in hard block.
bearish_impact: BigTech HOLDs (AMZN, GOOG) may slip to CAUTION

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
